title: Doctor i18n migration — knowledge lint batch (L3793-4697, ~29 sites)
description: Migrate the knowledge-lint region of doctor.ts (L3793-4697) from hardcoded English to translator-driven keys. Region covers: filesystem_edit_fallback (multi-arm), orphan_demote, stale_archive, pending_overdue, stable_id_duplicate, layer_mismatch, index_drift, underseeded, narrow_no_paths, relevance_paths_dangling, relevance_paths_drift, narrow_too_few. These are the lint #16-#27 family checks. Mechanically identical to TASK-02 — only the line range and the key set differ. Translator threading is reused (already added in TASK-02), so this task only modifies call sites without re-touching runDoctorReport signature.

files.target: L3793-4697 (approximately 29 okCheck/issueCheck call sites). Specific anchors: filesystem_edit_fallback arms L3793-3919, serve_lock advisory L3904-3919, stable_id_duplicate L4234-4248, layer_mismatch + index_drift L4255-4297 (note: relevance_paths_dangling also in this range but belongs to relevance group), underseeded L4555-4565, narrow_no_paths L4689-4697
files.change: Same migration pattern as TASK-02: thread the already-introduced Translator parameter `t` into each create*Check function in this range (most should already accept it from TASK-02's signature change, but per-function param adds happen task-local — verify and add as needed). Replace name + message + remediation literals with t() calls. Key naming: `doctor.check.<lint_kind>.{name|<arm>.{message|remediation|ok}}` matching the `code` field stem (e.g. `doctor.check.orphan_demote.*`, `doctor.check.relevance_paths_dangling.*`). Plural via `.singular`/`.plural` per planning-context D2.

implementation: Read packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L3793-4700 in chunks to enumerate the 29 call sites with their inspectFn → createFn → callSite chain.

For each create*Check function in this range: ensure it accepts the Translator parameter (passed by runDoctorReport per TASK-02). If function signature lacks it, add and update call site at L917+ checks array.
Replace English literals with t() calls per the convention. Use the same key namespace `doctor.check.<kind>.*` as TASK-02 — do not invent a new namespace.

Multi-arm functions (filesystem_edit_fallback has multiple branches based on orphan state) get .arm1/.arm2 suffixes or descriptive names like `.with_orphans`, `.complete`. Document the chosen arm names in this file's working comments.
Be especially careful with plural-form ternaries (`entry/entries`, `has/have`) — split into singular/plural keys per planning-context D2.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server typecheck` after this batch — green required.
Run `pnpm --filter @fenglimg/fabric-server test doctor` — expect some snapshot mismatches if any old snapshot captured English strings (acceptable; TASK-05 finalizes).
Commit: `feat(rc26): doctor i18n batch 2 — knowledge lint (TASK-03)`

reference.pattern: Identical to TASK-02 pattern. Lint #16-#27 namespace conventions from rc.4-rc.6 development.
reference.examples: TASK-02 establishes the migration pattern; TASK-03 applies it to the next batch.

rationale.chosen_approach: Mechanical batch identical to TASK-02 — only line range and key set change. Sequential after TASK-02 (same-file collision).
alternatives_considered: Merge into TASK-02 — rejected: 55 sites in one task is too large for clean review surface
Split further into TASK-03a/3b/3c — rejected: ~29 sites in one logical region (knowledge lints) is bisect-coherent
decision_factors: Knowledge-lint group is a natural code module
rationale.tradeoffs: None significant — task is a straightforward continuation of TASK-02.

success_metrics: Zero hardcoded English strings remain in L3793-4697 (grep for common phrases) returns 0

criteria: packages/server/src/services/doctor.ts L3793-4697 grep -c '"All relevance_paths globs resolve"' OR '"No .fabric/.serve.lock present"' (hardcoded English) returns 0
Commit msg: 'feat(rc26): doctor i18n batch 2 — knowledge lint (TASK-03)'
